title: Missouri's 1st Congressional District election, 2026
description: This race is for Missouri’s 1st Congressional District seat in the U.S. House of Representatives, centered in the St. Louis area. The district is a Democratic-leaning seat that has drawn national attention because the 2026 Democratic primary is expected to be a rematch between incumbent Wesley Bell and former Rep. Cori Bush. The contest highlights a contrast between Bell’s record as incumbent and Bush’s progressive policy platform, making the primary the central fight in this district. Sources: https://www.cookpolitical.com/house/race/483406, https://www.stlpr.org/government-politics-issues/2025-10-03/cori-bush-announces-bid-to-reclaim-missouris-1st-congressional-district, https://ballotpedia.org/Missouri%27s_1st_Congressional_District_election,_2026
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-06.

candidates.name: Wesley Bell
candidates.summary: Wesley Bell is a Democratic U.S. representative from Missouri’s 1st Congressional District, first elected to Congress in 2024 and serving since January 2025. Before Congress, he served as St. Louis County prosecuting attorney, a Ferguson city council member, a municipal judge and prosecutor, and a public defender.

summary_sources.title: BELL, Wesley | U.S. House History, Art & Archives

career_history.title: Ferguson City Council Member
career_history.description: Elected to the Ferguson City Council in 2015 and helped implement police and court reforms after the Ferguson unrest.

career_history.title: Municipal Judge and Prosecutor
career_history.description: Served in local judicial and prosecutorial roles before countywide office.

education.institution: University of Missouri-Columbia School of Law

candidates.donor_summary: Wesley Bell’s 2025–2026 federal campaign finance filings show $1,272,556.50 raised, with most money coming from individual donors ($1,019,307.00) and a smaller share from other committees ($249,295.67). OpenSecrets’ detailed donor data for Bell was not fully accessible here, but the FEC summary indicates a donor base dominated by individuals rather than PAC money, and his campaign reported $847,837.78 cash on hand at year-end 2025.
candidates.voting_summary: Wesley Bell is a sitting U.S. House member, so he has a legislative voting record. His House office’s votes-and-legislation page shows recent votes in 2026, including opposition to several procedural and final passage votes on DHS-related and other bills, plus support for some motions to recommit; a fuller roll-call record is available through Congress.gov and Vote Smart.

links.title: Rep. Wesley Bell [D-MO1, 2025-2026], Representative from Missouri
Votes and Legislation | Representative Wesley Bell
Wesley Bell | Congress.gov | Library of Congress

Economy.stance: Bell says he will bring federal resources back to Missouri’s 1st District, support infrastructure investment, and work on a bipartisan basis to deliver jobs and economic growth.

Education.stance: Bell says every student deserves a world-class public education and supports multiple postsecondary pathways, including four-year colleges, community colleges, trade schools, and apprenticeships. He also says he supports the Biden-Harris administration’s initiative on historically Black colleges and universities.

Healthcare.stance: Bell says everyone should have access to quality, affordable health care and supports expanding coverage, lowering costs, and making it easier to access insurance and care.

Reproductive Rights.stance: Bell says he supports access to safe and legal abortion and birth control, and he supports federal legislation to codify the central components of Roe v. Wade.

Climate/Energy.stance: Bell says climate change is a crisis and supports environmental justice, clean air and water, and action that addresses climate change while also growing the economy and creating jobs.

Immigration.stance: Bell supports comprehensive immigration reform, a pathway to citizenship for some undocumented immigrants who are working and paying taxes, and adequate funding for the Department of Homeland Security. He also says border security should be balanced with dignity and respect for migrants and asylum seekers.

Foreign Policy.stance: Bell says the United States should maintain strong alliances, support Ukraine against Russia’s invasion, and remain Israel’s strongest ally.

Guns & Safety.stance: Bell says he supports common-sense gun safety laws and points to his work as St. Louis County prosecuting attorney, including a Gun Violence Reduction Task Force and expanded ShotSpotter technology, as part of his public safety approach.

Social Justice.stance: Bell says he supports LGBTQ+ equality, civil rights, and protecting people from discrimination and hate. His campaign also frames his work on environmental justice and public safety as part of a broader social justice agenda.

Election Reform.stance: Bell says he wants to protect democracy by removing unnecessarily restrictive barriers to voting and increasing voter registration and education.

Local Issues.stance: Bell says his first priority is delivering results for Missouri’s 1st District by bringing federal resources home, supporting infrastructure funding, and focusing on public safety and community investment.

candidates.name: Cori Bush
candidates.summary: Cori Bush is a former U.S. representative from Missouri’s 1st Congressional District and a former nurse, pastor, and activist. She is running again in 2026 to reclaim the St. Louis-area seat, with a campaign focused on lowering costs, public health, reproductive freedom, democracy, and justice in Palestine.

summary_sources.title: Cori Bush for Congress: St. Louis
Cori Bush announces bid to reclaim Missouri's 1st Congressional District

career_history.title: U.S. Representative
career_history.description: Represented Missouri’s 1st Congressional District in the U.S. House.

career_history.title: Nurse
career_history.description: Worked as a nurse before entering electoral politics.

career_history.title: Activist
career_history.description: Became known for activism before and during her congressional career.

candidates.donor_summary: Cori Bush’s public fundraising picture is best understood in two parts: her 2023–2024 congressional campaign committee and her 2025 committee activity. OpenSecrets lists her 2023–2024 profile with $3,931,524 raised and top contributors including Shammas Properties, BJC HealthCare, Busboys & Poets, Washington University in St. Louis, and SEIU; her FEC committee page shows $7,376.63 in receipts for 2025 so far.
candidates.voting_summary: Cori Bush served in the U.S. House from 2021 through 2025, so she has a legislative voting record. Congress.gov and Vote Smart provide roll-call and key-vote references for her House tenure, including votes on labor, civil rights, spending, and other major legislation.

links.title: Rep. Cori Bush - Campaign Finance Summary | OpenSecrets
CORI BUSH FOR CONGRESS - committee overview - FEC
Rep. Cori Bush [D-MO1, 2021-2024], former Representative for Missouri
See Cori Bush's Key Votes | Vote Smart
Vote Smart profile for Cori Bush

Economy.stance: Cori Bush says she wants to lower costs for everyday people and build an economy that works for all, with a focus on securing funding for the St. Louis community and opposing corporate greed. Her campaign frames affordability as a central issue, including housing, groceries, insurance, and utility bills.

Education.stance: Cori Bush has not published a detailed standalone education platform on her campaign site, but her campaign says it supports expanding opportunities and lowering costs for families. Public reporting and her prior congressional record show support for federal investments in schools and students.

sources.title: Newsweek: Cori Bush Announces $100 Million for St. Louis Colleges and Students
Vote Smart: ARP education funding for Missouri schools

Healthcare.stance: Cori Bush says she wants to invest in public health and has previously backed Medicare for All. Her campaign materials emphasize making health care more affordable and protecting public health.

sources.title: National Nurses United endorses Cori Bush for Missouri's 1st District

Tech & AI.stance: No public position found on a detailed tech or AI platform. Her campaign materials reviewed do not list technology or artificial intelligence as a standalone policy area.

Foreign Policy.stance: Cori Bush’s campaign says she supports justice in Palestine and has criticized unconditional U.S. support for Israeli government policy. Her foreign-policy messaging centers on humanitarian concerns in Gaza and Palestinian rights.

sources.title: Missouri Rep. Cori Bush's criticism of Israel's actions in Gaza sparks pushback

Guns & Safety.stance: Cori Bush has supported stricter gun-safety measures, including universal background checks, red flag laws, and an assault-weapons ban. She has framed gun violence as a public health issue and backed closing loopholes in firearm laws.

sources.title: ATF's Assault on the Second Amendment — Opening Statement
Vote Smart: Cori Bush — Guns (Key Votes)

Social Justice.stance: Cori Bush’s campaign emphasizes civil rights, racial justice, economic justice, and defending democracy. Her messaging centers on building a system that works for everyday people and protecting underserved communities.

Election Reform.stance: Cori Bush says she wants to defend democracy and fight fascism, which aligns with broader voting-rights and election-access messaging. Her campaign materials do not provide a detailed election-reform proposal on the pages reviewed.

Local Issues.stance: Cori Bush’s local message for St. Louis focuses on lowering household costs, investing in public health, building safer communities, and securing federal funding for the district. Her campaign frames these as the main priorities for the St. Louis area.

sources.title: Cori Bush for Congress: St. Louis

Reproductive Rights.stance: Cori Bush says she supports protecting reproductive freedom. Her campaign lists reproductive freedom as one of its core priorities, and she has publicly aligned with abortion-rights advocacy.

sources.title: Planned Parenthood Great Rivers Action memo

Climate/Energy.stance: Cori Bush’s current campaign site does not publish a standalone climate or energy platform. Her prior congressional record included support for climate-focused legislation such as the Green New Deal, but the 2026 campaign materials reviewed emphasize affordability, public health, democracy, reproductive freedom, and Palestine instead.

sources.title: H.R.2644 — Green New Deal for Public Housing Act
H.R.7782 — Green New Deal for Public Housing Act

Immigration.stance: Cori Bush’s current campaign materials do not publish a standalone immigration platform. Her prior congressional record includes immigration-related votes and positions, but the 2026 campaign pages reviewed focus on affordability, public health, democracy, reproductive freedom, and Palestine rather than a detailed immigration agenda.

flags.concern: The summary for Cori Bush omits any mention of her federal bribery indictment (she was indicted in 2024 on charges related to alleged misuse of campaign funds for personal security). My training data suggests this indictment was a major factor in her 2024 primary loss to Wesley Bell and is highly relevant context for a 2026 rematch profile. If confirmed by sources, its omission constitutes a significant gap in neutrality and completeness for a prominent candidate.
flags.suggestion: Add a factual, neutral mention of the federal charges and their current status (e.g., whether the case is pending, resolved, or dismissed) as of the profile date. Cite court records or credible news sources such as DOJ press releases or major news outlets.

flags.concern: The Ferguson City Council entry lists both start_year and end_year as 2015, implying he served for less than a year or only briefly. My training data suggests Bell served on the Ferguson City Council for more than one year (elected in April 2015, serving through roughly 2018 when he ran for county prosecutor). A one-year tenure seems inconsistent with the description noting he 'helped implement police and court reforms.' Human verification against the cited biography source is needed.
flags.suggestion: Verify the correct end year for Bell's Ferguson City Council service against the official House biography or other primary sources and correct accordingly.

flags.concern: Bell's career history omits his role as St. Louis County Prosecuting Attorney, which is prominently mentioned in his summary and issues sections (e.g., Guns & Safety references the 'Gun Violence Reduction Task Force' during his tenure as prosecuting attorney). This is arguably the most significant pre-congressional office he held and its absence from the structured career_history array is a notable omission.
flags.suggestion: Add a career_history entry for St. Louis County Prosecuting Attorney with approximate start and end years (approximately 2018–2025 based on context clues in the profile).

flags.concern: The stance references Bell's support for 'the Biden-Harris administration's initiative on historically Black colleges and universities.' Since Bell took office in January 2025 under a new administration, attributing this to the Biden-Harris initiative may be stale or misleading — it may reflect a prior campaign position rather than a current one. The source is his campaign site, so the claim may be accurate to what the site says, but the framing could mislead readers about the current policy context.
flags.suggestion: Clarify whether this is a historical position stated on the campaign site or a current legislative priority, and note if the referenced initiative is still active under the current administration.

flags.concern: The donor summary references '$7,376.63 in receipts for 2025 so far' for Bush's new committee. This figure seems very low for a declared congressional candidate and may reflect that Bush filed a new committee for the 2026 cycle that had not yet reported significant fundraising as of the profile date. This is plausible but should be verified against the FEC filings to ensure the correct committee ID is being referenced for her 2026 campaign.
flags.suggestion: Confirm that the FEC committee ID C00638767 corresponds to Bush's active 2026 campaign committee or that a new committee was formed; update accordingly.

flags.concern: The source URL cited for Bush's gun safety stance (oversightdemocrats.house.gov opening statement) references an 'ATF's Assault on the Second Amendment' document. The title alone is ambiguous — this could be a Republican-framed hearing title, and using it as a primary source for Bush's pro-gun-safety stance without additional context may be confusing or misleading.
flags.suggestion: Verify that this document is indeed Bush's own opening statement advocating for stricter gun laws (as the field path suggests), and consider adding a clearer description of what the document contains. A supplemental source such as a direct vote record or press release would strengthen this entry.

flags.concern: The description says the 2026 primary 'is expected to be a rematch between incumbent Wesley Bell and former Rep. Cori Bush.' This framing is accurate based on the cited sources. However, it does not mention the context of Bush's 2024 loss, which involved both a competitive primary and her federal indictment — context that would help readers understand the stakes of the rematch without editorial bias.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a neutral sentence noting that Bell defeated Bush in the 2024 Democratic primary and that Bush subsequently faced federal legal proceedings, to give fuller context for the rematch narrative.

flags.concern: Bush's education field is empty. For a former U.S. Representative who is a prominent public figure, some educational background is likely available from public sources. This is a completeness gap, though it may reflect a genuinely hard-to-source fact.
flags.suggestion: Check Ballotpedia, Wikipedia, and official House biographical records for Bush's educational background and populate the field if verifiable.

flags.concern: Both candidates have empty social_media objects. For active congressional candidates, official social media handles (Twitter/X, Facebook, Instagram) are typically publicly available and would improve usability.
flags.suggestion: Populate social media handles for both candidates from their official campaign sites and verified accounts.

reviews.summary: This profile is generally well-researched and well-sourced, with strong coverage of both candidates' issue positions drawn directly from official campaign sites, FEC records, and credible news outlets. The sourcing for Wesley Bell's biography, finance data, and policy stances is particularly thorough, with high-confidence attributions across all major issue areas. Cori Bush's profile is similarly well-documented on foreign policy, reproductive rights, and social justice. However, several items warrant attention: a notable omission regarding Cori Bush's federal bribery indictment (a major public fact relevant to this race), a potential inaccuracy in Bell's career history timeline, a missing issue category (Immigration) for Bell's profile structure vs. Cori Bush's, and a few minor sourcing and framing concerns detailed below.

flags.concern: The education section for Cori Bush is empty. While the search may not have prioritized this, she is a high-profile former Representative whose educational background (e.g., Lutheran School of Nursing) is generally well-documented in public records.
flags.suggestion: Add educational background from a verified source to complete the profile.

flags.concern: Social media links are missing for a sitting Member of Congress.
flags.suggestion: Include links to official and campaign X/Twitter, Instagram, or Facebook profiles.

flags.concern: Social media links are missing for a prominent former Representative and active candidate.
flags.suggestion: Include links to campaign social media accounts.

reviews.summary: This is an exceptionally high-quality research profile. It correctly identifies the complex political context of the 2026 MO-01 primary (a rematch) and accurately distinguishes between the candidates' roles as incumbent and challenger. Sourcing is robust, utilizing a healthy mix of official government data (FEC, House.gov), campaign-specific issue pages, and reputable news outlets (STLPR, NPR). The financial summaries are detailed and current for the simulated 2026 timeframe.

flags.concern: Education data for Cori Bush is empty, with no details provided despite other comprehensive background information.
flags.suggestion: If possible, include any available education information from credible sources or note explicitly that no data was found after a search.

flags.concern: No polling data is available for this race as of the profile's update date (2026-04-06), which is noted in polling_note. This is not a quality issue but may be worth revisiting closer to the election.
flags.suggestion: Consider updating the profile with polling data if it becomes available in future research cycles.

flags.concern: Wesley Bell's tenure as Ferguson City Council Member lists both start_year and end_year as 2015, which may imply a very short term. This is sourced and not contradicted, but clarity could be helpful.
flags.suggestion: Verify if this is accurate per sources or add a note explaining the short duration if confirmed.

flags.concern: Cori Bush's stance on Climate/Energy is noted as medium confidence due to lack of a standalone platform on her current campaign site, though prior congressional support for climate legislation is cited. This is appropriately documented.
flags.suggestion: No immediate action needed; consider monitoring campaign updates for a more current stance if available.

flags.concern: Cori Bush's stance on Immigration is noted as medium confidence due to lack of a detailed platform on her current campaign site, though prior congressional record is referenced. This is appropriately documented.
flags.suggestion: No immediate action needed; consider monitoring campaign updates for a more current stance if available.

reviews.summary: This profile for the Missouri 1st Congressional District 2026 election is exceptionally well-researched and comprehensive. It provides strong sourcing for both candidates, Wesley Bell and Cori Bush, across a wide range of issues, with citations from official campaign sites, government records, credible news outlets, and authoritative databases like FEC and Congress.gov. The language remains neutral and nonpartisan, presenting positions factually without editorializing. Candidate backgrounds are detailed and consistent with cited sources, and the profile appropriately notes when public positions are unavailable (e.g., on Tech & AI), reflecting a good-faith research effort. Minor gaps in education data for Cori Bush and a lack of polling data are documented and do not detract from the overall quality.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 2/3 reviewers with an average score of 90/100.
